CALL FOR ENTRIES
UNTIL MAY 1, 2026

The international award for excellence in the design of spaces, products, landscapes and brands — open worldwide.

8

DAYS REMAINING

Deadline: May 1, 2026

SUBMIT NOW →

Your work deserves to be seen.

The GRANDS PRIX DU DESIGN Awards exist to put exceptional design on the international map. For 19 editions, we have celebrated the studios, architects, designers, and creators who push their discipline forward, giving them the recognition, visibility, and connections that follow.

An independent mark of excellence. A distinction that lasts.

Open to the world. Built for every discipline.

Whether you are an established firm, an emerging practice, a manufacturer, or a student, if you create spaces, products, landscapes, or brands, there’s a category for you.

Projects completed within the past 3 years are eligible (5 years for urban design and landscape). Submit one project or many. Each submission is independently evaluated.

IN THEIR OWN WORDS

Winners speak

“It’s an incredible honor. Being here in Montreal for my first awards ceremony and celebrating architecture and design at this level is truly special.”

KATIE KROLAC

AKB Architects · Toronto, Canada

→ WATCH THE VIDEO

“Winning in Montreal is especially meaningful. The city stands as a bridge between Europe and North America.”

MARK ENGLISH

Mark English Architects · San Francisco, USA

→ WATCH THE VIDEO

“It’s an incredible honor to be part of this event. To discover the work of others, get inspired, and share your own.”

MAURICIO VILLARREAL

PLACE · Portland, USA

→ WATCH THE VIDEO

A Prestigious International Jury of Experts

Submissions are evaluated by an international jury of 110+ leading experts in design, architecture, landscape, communication and the built environment — from prominent firms, academia and specialized media worldwide.

A distinction from the GRANDS PRIX DU DESIGN Awards signifies outstanding achievement and international recognition, enhancing your firm’s visibility among clients, partners and the global design community.

Among this year’s featured jurors:

	
Christophe Pillet
Designer, Agence Christophe Pillet
Paris, France
	
Casey Jones
Chief Design Officer, Perkins&Will
Chicago, USA
	
Jenchieh Hung & Kulthida Songkittipakdee
Co-Founders, HAS design and research
Bangkok, Thailand

Athens, Greece
	
Denys Lapointe
Chief Design Officer, BRP
Valcourt, Québec, Canada
	
Alain Gilles
Product & Furniture Designer, Alain Gilles / The Studio
Brussels, Belgium

Discover all winners →

Showcase your Projects on the International Stage

A distinction that strengthens credibility, visibility, and business development.

BUSINESS LEVERAGE

Use your distinction as a powerful development tool to generate new commissions, attract talent, and create strategic partnership opportunities.

INTERNATIONAL RECOGNITION

Have your work evaluated by an international jury of leading experts in architecture and design, and benefit from a distinction that immediately strengthens your professional credibility with clients and partners.

STRATEGIC VISIBILITY

Benefit from visibility that crosses borders — our bilingual French and English platforms connect your distinction with an international audience of specifiers, clients and potential partners.

LASTING VALUE

An award distinction continues to strengthen your reputation and positioning long after the competition, contributing to the growth and recognition of your firm over time.

SUBMIT A PROJECT →
Full pricing details

 

Submit your project in minutes.

Create your free account and complete your submission in just a few steps:

1. Add your firm information: name, main contact, and details

2. Select the discipline corresponding to your project

3. Enter your project name and choose your categories
(The project name can be modified until May 1)

4. Proceed with secure online payment

Once payment is confirmed, you will be able to upload or modify your information and files until May 1, 2026.
Your submission can be adjusted at any time until the deadline.

Need help? Contact us at info@int.design

Meadow House | Mark English Architects

AWARD ACCOLADES

© Joe Fletcher Photography

Following the registration period, the International Jury will conduct an online deliberation. The awarding of certifications, the announcement of finalists, the announcement of winners and the nomenclature of the prizes awarded will be as follows.
1. OFFICIAL SELECTION

————————————————

After the entry deadline, a first evaluation determines the OFFICIAL SELECTION of the GRANDS PRIX DU DESIGN Awards. This step selects qualified entries and prepares them for the international jury’s deliberation.

2. CERTIFICATIONS

————————————————

Each project or product is evaluated by the jury on a scale of 1 to 100 points. Certifications are awarded based on the average score obtained:

60 to 69: BRONZE certification
70 to 79: SILVER certification
80 to 100: GOLD certification

Certifications are granted to projects that, according to the jury’s evaluation, meet or exceed the competition’s standards of excellence.

3. FINALISTS

————————————————-

Projects or products that receive a Gold certification — with a score between 80 and 100 — become finalists and are in the running for the Grand Winner title.
The official announcement of the finalists will be published online.

4. WINNER

————————————————

The GRAND WINNER title is awarded to the project or product that received highest score (above 80) in its category..

PEOPLE’S CHOICE AWARD
All projects will be submitted to a public vote.
A People’s Choice Award will be presented in each discipline , discipline to the project or product that receives the highest number of online votes..

AWARDS OF THE YEAR
An « Award of the Year » trophy is presented to projects that have particularly distinguished themselves within certain competition disciplines, among the Grand Winners.

These distinctions are granted by the international jury, which deliberates to select its top picks among the most remarkable achievements of the edition.

The announcement of the winners will be made during the two Gala evenings.

Submit your project or product

CHEA SINENSIS Experience Center, Hangzhou
| Nature Times Art Design

EVALUATION CRITERIA

© Nature Times Art Design

Projects submitted to the GRANDS PRIX DU DESIGN Awards are evaluated by an international jury of experts through a structured, rigorous, and impartial process, aligned with the standards of leading international design competitions.
Evaluation Process

INDEPENDENT EVALUATION

Each project is independently reviewed by multiple international jurors.

STRUCTURED FRAMEWORK

The evaluation is based on a structured framework that analyzes projects according to their intent, execution, and impact.

CONSOLIDATED RESULTS

Cross-analysis and result consolidation ensure a balanced, consistent, and rigorous assessment.

What the Jury Evaluates

Each project is assessed holistically. The jury evaluates the project’s ability to express a clear intent, provide a relevant response, and generate real value for its users and environment.


✧

DESIGN EXCELLENCE

Does the project demonstrate a clear vision and strong design mastery, both in concept and execution?

○

FUNCTION AND EXPERIENCE

Does the project effectively fulfill its purpose and meaningfully enhance the user experience?

⬦

INNOVATION

Does the project offer a relevant approach, a new perspective, or added value beyond existing solutions?

◎

IMPACT

Does the project contribute positively to its environment on a social, cultural, economic, or environmental level?

◉

COHERENCE AND INTENT

Does the project clearly express a guiding idea and a consistent vision?

□

QUALITY OF EXECUTION

Do the level of detail, materials, and execution demonstrate overall mastery?

⌖

CONTEXTUAL RELEVANCE

Does the project respond intelligently to its environment, users, and contemporary challenges?

▣

CLARITY OF PRESENTATION

Does the submission effectively communicate the project through images, plans, and descriptions?

EXAMPLE EVALUATION QUESTION
Does the project accurately fulfill its initial intent while delivering tangible value to its users and context?

SCALE: 0 TO 10


PROCESS AT A GLANCE

● Evaluation by multiple international jurors

● Independent and cross-reviewed analysis

● No contact between jurors and participants

How to Maximize your Chances

The highest-rated projects are those that clearly communicate their intent, demonstrate strong decisions, and present a coherent, well-executed vision.

→

CLARIFY YOUR INTENT FROM THE START

Clearly explain the core idea of the project.

≡

STRUCTURE YOUR NARRATIVE

Present the project logically: context, intent, solution, result.

✎

DEMONSTRATE YOUR DECISIONS

Explain design choices, not just the final outcome.

◿

SUPPORT YOUR CLAIMS

Back up impact, performance, or innovation with concrete elements.

▢

SELECT VISUALS WITH INTENTION

Use clear, legible, and representative images.

—

BE CONCISE AND FOCUSED

A clear and targeted presentation facilitates evaluation.

NOTE

These criteria provide a general evaluation framework and may vary depending on the category.

Each project is assessed as a whole, based on its context, intent, and execution.


Submit a project that demonstrates real value.

Get your project recognized internationally.

Special Awards are offered at a lower cost and are intended to reward specific features of a project or product.

BENEFIT FROM A DISCOUNT

ADDITIONAL PROJECTS/PRODUCTS

Enter more than one (1) project/product and get a 20% discount on your additional projects/products
(2 and more).

ADDITIONAL CATEGORIES

Enter your project/product in more than one (1) category and get a 50% discount on your additional
categories (2 and more).

BONUS

INT.DESIGN MAGAZINE

Your participation in the contest includes an annual subscription to INT.DESIGN magazine.

Shipping and handling included in Canada.
Candidates outside Canada who wish to receive a copy of the magazine are required to pay only the shipping and handling fees.

YOU DON’T HAVE TO FINALIZE YOUR REGISTRATION IN ORDER TO TAKE ADVANTAGE OF THE REDUCED RATE!

Enter the name of your project/product (which can be modified up to the closing date of the call for applications), select the discipline and categories you are interested in and make your payment before the discounted rate date.
You will then have until the final registration date to provide all your documents (photos, texts, PDF) and complete the registration.

Submit your project or product

Whistling Wind Island | Akb Architects

FAQs

© Doublespace

DATE AND FEES
What is the cost of entry?
+  

A: Entry cost varies depending on the date you make your registration, the category and the candidate status (professional or student), the number of projects or products submitted and the number of categories in which the project or product is submitted.

View entry pricing here

Is there a student entry fee?
+  

A: We offer a student-friendly entry fee to apply in many categories opened to students of post-secondary level enrolled in a contest-related discipline program.

View entry pricing here

Rates are set in which currency?
+  

A: Rates are listed in American dollars (USD) for international participants and in Canadian dollars (CAD) for Canadian residents.

May I cancel or postpone my entry? Will I be refunded?
+  

A: You may cancel your entry by sending an email to info@int.design. However, postponement to a later edition is not an option and no refunds will be issued under no circumstances.

What are the deadlines?
+  

A: See entry deadline calendar and pricing scale here.

Must my entry be completed prior to the deadline date for the reduced rate to apply?
+  

A: No. You simply need to process the payment prior to the reduced rate deadline date. You will have until the end of submissions to package your files and complete your entry.

May I ask for extra time to complete my entry?
+  

A: Entries are sent over to jury members once the entry deadline has passed. No extension will therefore be granted for incomplete entries.

PROJECT OR PRODUCT
Can I submit multiple projects/products in one same registration?
+  

A: Yes. The portal permits multiple entries of projects or products in one same registration and without limitation.

Can I submit a product series as a single entry?
+  

A: Yes. It is possible to submit a series of products as a single entry, provided that the elements demonstrate strong design consistency and clearly belong to one coherent set.
Your entry must specify that it is a series, include one image showing the series as a whole, and provide visuals of each main element. The entire series will be evaluated as a single project, and the jury will consider the overall coherence: a weaker element may negatively impact the final result.
If the coherence is not sufficiently strong, or if each product can be judged autonomously, we recommend submitting multiple individual entries.

Can I submit a project/product to a Special Award category only?
+  

A: No. A project/product must be submitted in a standard category to be eligible for entry in a Special Award category.

Can I enter a project/product I submitted last year again this year?
+  

A: Yes. If the project or product is always admissible to other conditions, you may submit it again. We encourage you to add updated data when available.

Can I submit my project/product again this year if it was awarded a GRANDS PRIX DU DESIGN last year?
+  

A: Yes. If your project or product has been modified since winning, it is again admissible to the contest.

Can I submit a project/product that has already been awarded by another contest?
+  

A: Yes. You can submit projects/products that have been entered and/or awarded by other contests.

Can I submit a project/product even though it is already online on my Website or must it be an exclusivity?
+  

A: You may submit a project or product already online. Our admission conditions do not limit you to submit in regard to its visibility status. You may submit projects or products showcased, showed online, printed, or published on any type of support.

Must my project/product be designed or made in Canada to be eligible?
+  

A: No. Our contest is open to projects and products designed and made anywhere around the World.

What kind of projects/products can be submitted to the contest?
+  

A: All types of projects in architecture, interior design, construction, real estate development, urban design, landscape architecture, industrial design, product design, communication, branding, art and photography can be submitted to the contest.

Must my project/product be realized to be eligible?
+  

A: Entries must be projects that are completed or in process and products that are available on the market, except for entries to Concept Presentation categories.

For how many years can a project/product be eligible for the contest?
+  

A: Projects must have been completed in the past three (3) years, or five (5) years in regard to urban design, landscape architecture and landscaping projects to be eligible.

Is a client discharge approval mandatory to submit a project or product?
+  

A: Client approval is generally recommended when entering a professional competition. In most cases, small customers will be thrilled to receive an award. As per larger institutions, bigger brands and multinationals, certain bureaucratic processes could limit your participation. We therefore recommend notifying customers. In our experience, several large customers will not only encourage designers to participate, they will also cover the registration fees. A rewarded project/product gives interesting visibility to both the customer and designer. In summary, seeking client approval is recommended, yet not required. It is however imperative that, in the event of the project/product being awarded, the designers have had publication rights released.

INSCRIPTION PROCEDURE
How may I register?
+  

Entries must be submitted online. Click here to register.

What are the steps to submitting an entry?
+  
Create your free account: Click on My account in the top right corner of the site;
Log in with your email and password, and you will be directed to your Dashboard;
Choose Register to GRANDS PRIX DU DESIGN;
Click on Participate in the GRANDS PRIX DU DESIGN Awards banner;
Fill out the required information about your firm;
Choose the discipline in which you want to register your project or product!
Fill in the required information about your project/product and choose the category(ies) you are interested in;
Proceed to payment;
Deposit your Digital media: photos, texts and additional documentation;
Validate your information and Good Luck!

 

What data and documents will I need to submit upon registration?
+  

A: Here is a list of what you will need to provide:

Contact information for the firm, professional or student submitting the application (required)
Name of the project/product (required)
Project/Product Status: Completed / Under Construction / Concept Stage (required)
Preferred language for inscription on your plaque or trophy if you become a winner: French or English (required)
Name of firm(s), professional(s), consortium or student(s) exactly as it should appear on your certificate, plaque, and/or trophy. (Maximum 60 characters) (required)
Name, website and category of professionals who collaborated on the project/product (optional) At a minimum, list your firm.
*If your firm is eligible for the Emerging Talent Award, resume or bio of the designer(s). (required if eligible)
Name of project client, product manufacturer or student’s school (required) (check confidential box if necessary)
Choice of discipline and category(ies) you wish to enter (required)
Up to 10 images* of the project/product. 3 to 5 images must have a minimum resolution of 300 DPI and a dimension of at least 10 inches on one side. These images may be published in the INT.DESIGN magazine if your project is selected as a winner (required)
Any other additional document deemed relevant (layout plan, technical drawing, final renderings, section and elevation, three-dimensional representation, site plan, specifications, multipage PDF, explanatory video). (optional)
Explanatory texts of the project/product for your promotion of 3,000 characters including spaces  (required)

a) Your promotional text will be used in our communications and on the contest website.

b) As for your text for the Jury, the project description should describe, among other things: the objectives and how they were achieved, the design challenges, the technological and/or material innovations, the social and environmental impact and for the products: the dimensions, the materials, the manufacturing techniques, the manufacturing process. Please refer to the jury’s evaluation criteria to develop a strong project description.

How will I know if my entry is duly completed and how may I remedy if it is not?
+  

You may check the state of your registration at any moment by logging in to our members section with your username and password and by choosing “My projects”. NOTE: An entry left incomplete upon registration closing date (missing texts, images, or other required documents improperly uploaded), your project or product will not get proper judgement by our jury. Although we will try our best to communicate with you in case of an incomplete application, we are not held responsible for incomplete entries. No refund will be granted for incomplete applications once the registration period closed.

Must I pay registration fees to have access to uploading my entry data (texts and photos)?
+  

Yes, registration fees must be paid to gain access to the platform to deposit or update your documentation at any moment, prior to registration closing date.

I am having problems connecting to the Website. What should I try?
+  

This usually occurs when an error was made in your password or with the email address provided when registering on the INT.design portal. Try resetting the pa

About
Disciplines & Categories
Certifications & Winners
Gala
Press info
Submit 
My account
 EN

Cirra | Eureka

CATEGORIES
Architecture
+  
Commercial & Hospitality Building
+  
Retail & Small Commercial Building
+  
Buildings for small- to mid-size retail and neighborhood services.
Restaurant, Bar & Nightlife Building
 
Market & Shopping Center
+  
Public markets and multi-tenant shopping centers, including malls. Excludes standalone shops (see Retail & Small Commercial).
Hotel & Resort
 
Office Building
+  
Buildings primarily for office use: towers, HQs, campuses, coworking. If substantially mixed, see Mixed-Use Building.
Mixed-Use Building
+  
Buildings combining two or more uses (e.g., retail + housing, office + hotel).
Factory & Warehouse
+  
Production, assembly, storage, or distribution facilities (including logistics hubs)
Agricultural Building
+  
Buildings for agricultural production, livestock, and processing: barns, livestock buildings, greenhouses, equipment sheds, silos, dairies / cheese plants, winery cellars, and post-harvest facilities (grading / packing).
Real Estate Sales Center
+  
Temporary or permanent spaces designed to market a real estate development: reception, model displays, presentation rooms, digital experience
Institutional, Civic & Education Building
+  
Library, Community & Cultural Center
 
School & Preschool Education Building
+  
Preschool, primary and secondary schools — new buildings, additions or campuses.
Higher Education & Research Building
+  
Universities, colleges and specialized schools: teaching buildings, libraries and research buildings.
Hospital & Healthcare Building
+  
Healthcare facilities — hospitals, clinics and care centers
Museum & Gallery
 
Government & Civic Building
+  
City hall, courthouse, public service centers.
Theater & Performance Hall
 
Sports, Leisure & Wellness Building
+  
Sports & Recreation Center
+  
Gymnasium, arena, aquatic center, ski lodge, fitness center, indoor track, climbing gym, ice rink, multi-sport fields and courts, skatepark
Wellness Center & Spa
+  
Thermal resorts, bathhouses, saunas and hammams, medispas / medical-aesthetic centers, floatation baths, thalassotherapy, wellness retreats, and recovery centers.
Residential Building
+  
Private House — Small & Medium (≤ 185 m² / ≤ 2,000 sq ft)
 
Private House — Large (> 185 m² / > 2,000 sq ft)
 
Multi-Unit Housing — Low Rise (1–5 floors)
 
Apartment & Condo — Mid / High-Rise (5+ floors)
 
House Renovation
+  
Partial or complete transformation of an existing residence.
Cottage & Country House
 
Affordable Housing
 
Prestige House
 
Prefabricated / Modular House
 
Micro House (≤ 50 m² / ≤ 500 sq ft)
 
House Extension
+  
An extension added to an existing home.
Other categories in architecture
+  
Concept & Unbuilt
+  
Unrealized or conceptual architectural projects.
Façade
+  
Facades of residential, commercial, institutional, and mixed-use buildings.
Infrastructure
+  
Bridges, tunnels, utilities and transportation structures.
Temporary Installation
+  
Temporary or ephemeral architectural structures.
Continuing Value (≥ 20 years)
+  
Buildings demonstrating lasting architectural value after 20+ years.
Open Category (Architecture)
+  
Projects that don’t fit other categories.
AI‑Assisted Architectural Project
+  
Built or conceptual buildings where AI tools played a material role in ideation, visualization, layout / optimization or documentation.
Special Award
+  
Architecture + Art & Artistic Integration
+  
Highlights the integration of art into architecture—from co-design to in-situ works—to enrich narrative, use and identity.
Architecture + Interdisciplinary Collaboration
+  
Rewards effective co-design processes bringing together designers, engineers, users and partners.
Architecture + Color & Atmospheres
+  
Recognizes expressive, well‑controlled use of color, daylight and textures to shape ambiance.
Architecture + Lighting
+  
Note: If lighting is a significant component of the project, also submit it under the “Lighting (Projects)” discipline.
Architecture + Stair
+  
Showcases the stair as a structuring element: spatial quality, safety, crafted detail and user experience.
Architecture + Ceiling
+  
Highlights ceiling quality (relief, technical systems, acoustics, light) and its contribution to identity.
Architecture + Site & Landscape Integration
+  
Assesses building–landscape integration: siting / massing with topography, views / access, ground / material continuity and water management — across all typologies.
Architecture + Wood
+  
Rewards structural relevance and architectural expression of wood, with durable implementation.
Architecture + Materials
+  
Salutes coherent material / structure / envelope integration (metal, concrete, glass, stone, bio‑based) and execution quality.
Architecture + Representation & Narrative
+  
Recognizes clarity of plans, sections, axonometrics, models and visuals (photos / renders) in communicating the project.
Architecture + Small Budget / Big Impact
+  
Rewards spatial and technical excellence achieved with limited resources (indicate total cost and, if possible, cost / m²).
Adaptive Reuse, Salvage & Heritage
+  
Honors transformation of existing buildings, component / material reuse and heritage conservation / valorization.
Innovation & Technologies
+  
Rewards innovative construction (prefabrication / DFMA, digital fabrication), smart systems, digital twin and AI.
Social Impact & Accessibility
+  
Assesses inclusion, universal accessibility, safety, well‑being and tangible community benefits.
Sustainability, Low‑Carbon & Resilience
+  
Assesses energy / carbon (operational & embodied), circularity / reuse, LCA, user comfort and adaptation to risks (heat, flooding, extremes).
Interior Design
+  
Office
+  
Office: Small & Medium (≤ 1,860 m² / ≤ 20,000 sq ft)
 
Office: Large (> 1,860 m² / > 20,000 sq ft)
 
Office: Brand & Client Experience
+  
Reception, client journey, experience centers and showrooms — brand storytelling and hospitality.
Office: Agile (ABW) & Co-working Environment
+  
Activity-based workplaces (unassigned seating; collaboration / focus / social zones) and co-working with shared services.
Meeting & Collaboration Space
+  
Meeting rooms, project / war rooms and hybrid suites enabling co-creation (AV / IT, acoustics, flexibility).
Workplace Dining & Social Hub
+  
Coffee points, dining areas and social hubs integrated into the workplace.
Retail
+  
Retail: Small & Medium (≤ 500 m² / ≤ 5,400 sq ft)
 
Retail: Large (> 500 m² / > 5,400 sq ft)
 
Retail: Food
+  
Food market, specialty grocery, bakery / pastry, wine & spirits.
Retail: Beauty, Health & Wellness
+  
Cosmetics, fragrance, hair / esthetic care, health & wellness retail / clinics.
Real Estate Sales Center / Sales Office
+  
Interiors designed to market a real estate development
Retail: Pop-up, Kiosk & Exhibition Space
+  
Temporary pavilion / stand, in-store / gallery pop-ups, mall / fair kiosks, brand stands.

+

+

Culture: Library & Media Library

+

+

Bedroom + bathroom + walk-in / closet.

+  
Bedrooms, play / study areas.
Home Office
+  
Integrated workspace for remote work.

+  
Lobbies, lounges / terraces, gyms and shared areas.

Aircraft cabins, train / metro / bus cars, ships / ferries

Google Analytics sets this cookie to calculate visitor, session and campaign data and track site usage for the site's analytics report. The cookie stores information anonymously and assigns a randomly generated number to recognise unique visitors.

_gid
Duration
1 day
Description
Google Analytics sets this cookie to store information on how visitors use a website while also creating an analytics report of the website's performance. Some of the collected data includes the number of visitors, their source, and the pages they visit anonymously.

1 minute

Cookie

Duration
3 months
Description
Facebook sets this cookie to display advertisements when either on Facebook or on a digital platform powered by Facebook advertising after visiting the website.
Cookie
_hjSessionUser_*
Duration
1 year
Description
Hotjar sets this cookie to ensure data from subsequent visits to the same site is attributed to the same user ID, which persists in the Hotjar User ID, which is unique to that site.
Cookie
_hjFirstSeen
Duration
1 hour
Description
Hotjar sets this cookie to identify a new user’s first session. It stores the true/false value, indicating whether it was the first time Hotjar saw this user.
Cookie
_hjSession_*
Duration
1 hour
Description
Hotjar sets this cookie to ensure data from subsequent visits to the same site is attributed to the same user ID, which persists in the Hotjar User ID, which is unique to that site.
